title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Matt Turner made key saves for the Revolution, recovering from a midweek mistake against Nashville.
The Revolution capitalized on turnovers, particularly with Luca Langoni's goal after pressuring Minnesota's midfield.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted the SNL season 51 finale on May 16, with Paul McCartney as the musical guest.
Ferrell's notable performance featured a cold open as the ghost of Jeffrey Epstein alongside James Austin Johnson's Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including "Days We Left Behind" and "Band on the Run," featuring Chad Smith.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 is part of the Sail250 Tall Ships tour, celebrating the United States’ 250th anniversary from July 11-16.
Previous Sail Boston events took place in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, drawing millions of spectators.
The 1992 and 2000 events saw protests citing tall ships as reminders of slave transportation, contrasting the aim of cultural diversity celebration.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to New England Patriots for rookie minicamp, despite an offseason controversy.
The vibe in the Patriots' building has improved, and Vrabel's energy is noted as consistent with last season.
Patriots to face Chiefs, Seahawks, Lions, Bears, and Bills; Vrabel's coaching deemed crucial for another playoff run.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run homer in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 victory over the Braves.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, yielding two runs and four hits, with Aroldis Chapman securing the save.
The Red Sox secured their first comeback win trailing after seven innings in 2026, aiming for a series win in Sunday's game.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list with sports hernia, may need surgery.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on Red Sox roster.
Story batting .206 and has committed six errors in 39 games this season.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died after his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus in Marlborough, New Hampshire.
The crash involved a school bus carrying 13 students; no serious injuries were reported among the students.
The incident is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police with assistance from Marlborough police and fire officials.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski delivered a game-winning hit in the 10th inning, leading the Braves to a 3-2 victory over the Red Sox.
Boston Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs and struck out six over five innings without surrendering a walk.
Marcelo Mayer hit a solo home run for the Red Sox, but the team struggled offensively, leaving seven on base and tying the game in the seventh.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots unlikely to reunite with Stefon Diggs due to pending A.J. Brown trade.
Trade for A.J. Brown complicates potential return of Diggs, impacting Patriots' cap space.
Stefon Diggs expressed interest in returning to Patriots; played 52% of snaps last season.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, reported missing in Dorchester was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave.
Boston police describe LaGrant-Gomes as 5'8", thin, with brown eyes and black hair, often visiting Columbia Road and Downtown Crossing.
Public urged to report information on LaGrant-Gomes to 911 or District B-3 detectives; anonymous tips can be submitted via CrimeStoppers.
